Task 10: Port textured sprite pipeline to Vulkan

Implement the textured sprite VkPipeline and drawSprite() in VulkanRenderer:

- Created textured sprite graphics pipeline using textured.vert.spv /
  textured.frag.spv with vec2 position + vec2 texcoord vertex input,
  64-byte push constants (mat4 projection), two descriptor set layouts
  (set 0: lighting UBO, set 1: texture samplers + TextureFlags UBO),
  alpha blending, and dynamic viewport/scissor.

- Implemented drawSprite() matching GLRenderer's UV and vertex math:
  builds 6-vertex textured quad from frame rect, uploads to per-frame
  dynamic buffer, binds pipeline, pushes projection, binds descriptor
  sets, and issues vkCmdDraw(6).

- Created dummy lighting UBO (zeroed, 4096 bytes) with dedicated
  descriptor pool and pre-allocated descriptor set as placeholder for
  the lighting system.

- Created flat normal texture (1x1 RGBA: 128,128,255,255) and
  TextureFlags UBO (uHasNormalMap=0) as defaults for the normal map
  placeholder binding.

- Updated texture descriptor set layout to include binding 2 (uniform
  buffer for TextureFlags) matching the textured.frag shader, and
  expanded the descriptor pool to include UBO descriptors.

- Added getOrCreateTextureDescriptorSet() helper that allocates and
  writes descriptor sets with diffuse texture, normal map, and
  TextureFlags UBO, using the existing descriptor set cache.

v0.15.0 — Multi-Mind Runtime (Phase 1) (#6)

* step 1: shared types for multi-mind runtime

Add MindContext, MindIdentity, MindStatus, MindRecord types.
Update AppConfig to v2 with minds[] array and activeMindId.
Add mind: namespace to ElectronAPI, update chat: to take mindId.
Create InternalMindContext in main/services/mind/types.ts.
Deprecate old agent: namespace and AppConfigV1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 2: ConfigService evolution with v1→v2 migration (TDD)

ConfigService now loads/saves AppConfig v2 with minds[] array.
Migrates v1 { mindPath } to v2 { minds: [{id, path}] } transparently.
Static generateMindId() creates folder-basename + 4-hex-char IDs.
Deduplicates minds with same path on load.
10 tests covering migration, defaults, corrupt data, dedupe.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 3: CopilotClientFactory replaces SdkLoader singleton (TDD)

Instance-based factory creates one CopilotClient per mind path.
Extract sdkImport.ts for testable SDK module loading.
5 tests: create, isolation, caching, destroy, error handling.
SdkLoader kept temporarily — will be removed in composition step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 4: IdentityLoader returns MindIdentity {name, systemMessage} (TDD)

Load() now returns MindIdentity with extracted name from H1 heading.
Falls back to folder basename when no H1 exists.
6 tests covering name extraction, fallback, agent files, null cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 5: ExtensionLoader stateless refactor (TDD)

loadTools() now returns {tools, loaded} — caller stores per-mind.
cleanup() is static, takes loaded extensions as parameter.
No internal state between calls — safe for multi-mind concurrent use.
9 tests covering statelessness, error handling, cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 6: ViewDiscovery per-mind storage (TDD)

Views and watchers now keyed by mindPath (Map<string, views[]>).
scan() adds to the map instead of replacing all views.
getViews/getViewData/refreshView/sendAction accept mindPath.
removeMind() clears views + stops watchers for that mind.
Decoupled from ChatService — uses ViewRefreshHandler interface.
11 tests covering per-mind isolation, scan, data, watchers, cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 7: MindManager — core multi-mind service (TDD)

Aggregate root owns Map<mindId, InternalMindContext>.
loadMind: validates, creates client, loads identity/extensions, creates session.
unloadMind: cleanup, destroy, persist, active mind fallback.
restoreFromConfig: partial restore (bad paths don't block others).
recreateSession: new conversation support.
Deduplicates same-path loads. EventEmitter for lifecycle events.
20 tests covering load, unload, dedupe, restore, shutdown, isolation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 8: ChatService refactored to thin MindManager wrapper (TDD)

Removed setMindPath/getMindPath, getOrCreateSession, SdkLoader import.
All methods now take mindId, delegate to MindManager for sessions.
sendMessage streams events, cancelMessage aborts, newConversation recreates.
listModels gets client from MindManager. No session/extension ownership.
6 new tests covering send, cancel, newConversation, listModels, error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 9: IPC adapters refactored for multi-mind

New mind.ts: mind:add, mind:remove, mind:list, mind:setActive, mind:selectDirectory.
Updated chat.ts: all handlers take mindId, emit events with mindId scope.
Updated genesis.ts: uses MindManager.loadMind instead of ChatService.setMindPath.
Updated lens.ts: all handlers accept optional mindId, resolve via MindManager.
Agent.ts kept temporarily for backward compat — will be removed in composition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 10: Preload updated with mind: namespace and mindId params

New mind: namespace (add, remove, list, setActive, selectDirectory, onMindChanged).
Chat methods now take mindId instead of conversationId.
Lens methods accept optional mindId.
Agent namespace kept for backward compat (deprecated).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* step 11: Renderer state & reducer for multi-mind (TDD)

AppState: minds[], activeMindId, messagesByMind (per-mind message storage).
New actions: SET_MINDS, SET_ACTIVE_MIND, ADD_MIND, REMOVE_MIND.
CHAT_EVENT routes by mindId. REMOVE_MIND falls back or shows landing.
44 tests (was 38 — added mind management tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address all adversarial review findings

- utils.py: rename get_project_root() -> get_app_data_dir(), returns
  Path.home() / '.seakarr' so pip-installed paths work correctly

- search.py: return list[dict] | None; None on API failure (transient
  error), [] on successful-but-empty results so callers can distinguish

- filter.py: remove premature filter_by_search_type from build_candidates
  pipeline (ran before disc merging, incorrectly eliminated multi-disc
  albums); pick_best_directory now validates all merged disc groups and
  best_single against _valid_for_type; fallback returns None if nothing
  valid

- scanner.py: skip disc subdirs (CD1/CD2) in main scan loop to avoid
  returning (Album, CD1) as artist/album; combine direct_audio + disc_audio
  for accurate track count; OSError guards on iterdir(); use
  contextlib.suppress; _album_info_from_directory searches disc subdirs
  for tag files

- organizer.py: pre-detect duplicate basenames (two discs can both have
  track01.flac) and use parent/filename when duplicates exist; cleanup is
  deepest-first and includes parent dirs of disc subdirs

- download.py: fix cancel race (only append to cancelled after confirmed
  cancel); fix untracked early-return (cancel + cleanup before returning
  when not active); fix polling hang (remove continue from except, init
  current_by_id before try); move speed_checked=True inside 'if speeds:'
  so re-check is not skipped when no in-progress transfers found yet;
  cancel untracked failed filenames at normal exit

- database.py: add add_download_batch() for atomic batch insert

- cli.py: update import to get_app_data_dir; gate organize_files call
  inside res.success check; validate len(moved) before recording to DB;
  use add_download_batch for atomic recording; guard is_downloaded and
  is_skipped with 'album is not None'; handle None from network_search
  and user_browse (API error -> skip without penalising DB); distinguish
  API error (None) from empty results ([]) in both search paths

- filter.rs: wrap unsafe ops in score_vectors_avx2 in explicit unsafe {}
  block (Rust 2024 edition: unsafe fn no longer implicitly creates an
  unsafe context); hsum_pd body is clean (pure register intrinsics, no
  raw pointer ops, so no wrapper needed)
- filter.rs: add 12 unit tests for score_vectors_with_metric covering
  all three metrics (IP/Cosine/L2) at short (scalar path) and long
  (AVX2 path + scalar tail) dimensions, including zero-vector edge cases
- release.yml: fix macOS x86_64 linker error by routing x86_64 matrix
  rows to macos-13 (Intel runner, installs x86_64 Python) and aarch64
  rows to macos-latest (ARM64); add fail-fast: false to macOS strategy
  so one failure no longer cascades and cancels all other macOS jobs
- release.yml: add FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true env to
  suppress Node.js 20 deprecation warnings from checkout/setup-python/
  upload-artifact/download-artifact actions
- ci.yml: add same FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24 env; add
  cargo check -q with RUSTFLAGS=-D warnings step to catch future
  E0133-style warnings as errors in every CI run

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

* fix: remove per-tick timeout that killed Discord gateway after 30 seconds

The PluginBackgroundTaskRunner applied a 30-second handler timeout to
ExecuteAsync via CancellationTokenSource.CancelAfter. This killed the
Discord gateway task 30 seconds after startup — only the startup
announcement got through before the task died.

The previous attempt to fix this (using PluginStopping token) didn't
work because the tickCts cancellation propagated through the
cancellationToken parameter regardless.

Root fix: ExecuteAsync now receives the plain shutdown token with no
per-tick timeout. Background tasks like the Discord gateway run for
their natural lifetime. The circuit breaker still handles crashes.

Also moves slash command registration from OnStartupAsync to
ExecuteAsync (after ConnectAsync), because RegisterSlashCommandsAsync
needs an active gateway connection to resolve the guild.

* fix: resolve doubled Discord messages and show external input in chat

Two Discord plugin bugs fixed:

1. Agent messages appeared with doubled content in Discord. The
   PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer accumulated V3 streaming
   deltas AND the final assistant.message (which contains the complete
   text), concatenating them into doubled output. Fixed by adding an
   IsCompleteMessage flag to AgentMessageChunkEvent — set true only for
   V3 assistant.message events. The buffer now skips the final event's
   text when deltas were already buffered. Buffer key changed from
   sessionId to (sessionId, agentId) for multi-agent isolation.

   SignalR handler unconditionally ignored all UserMessage events,
   assuming they were added optimistically by the web UI. External
   messages (Discord) bypassed the web UI and were lost. Fixed with a
   counted-multiset dedup: ChatView.trackSentMessage() after successful
   API delivery, SignalR handler consumeSentMessage() to distinguish

* fix: use authoritative V3 complete text to prevent cross-turn Discord message duplication

The PluginObserverBridgeService message buffer had a race condition caused
by BroadcastingSessionEventSink's fire-and-forget dispatch pattern. When
the assistant.message (complete text) event won the thread pool race and
processed before all deltas, orphaned deltas accumulated in the buffer and
contaminated the next turn's output — producing doubled/corrupt messages.

Fix: when IsCompleteMessage is true, always use the event's authoritative
complete text and discard buffered deltas. This matches what
BroadcastingSessionEventSink already does for message persistence.

Added regression test simulating the race: final processed before deltas,
orphaned deltas present when next turn arrives.

commit.message:
Task 9: Implement Vulkan texture loading pipeline

Implement loadTexture() in VulkanRenderer with full Vulkan texture
upload pipeline:
- Check path-based texture cache to avoid reloading
- Load images via stb_image (force RGBA), 2x2 magenta fallback on failure
- Create VkImage (R8G8B8A8_SRGB, SAMPLED|TRANSFER_DST) via VMA
- Staging buffer upload with layout transitions
  (UNDEFINED -> TRANSFER_DST -> SHADER_READ_ONLY)
- VkImageView (2D, RGBA) and VkSampler (nearest filter, clamp-to-edge)
- Incrementing TextureHandle IDs stored in cache

Infrastructure added:
- VkDescriptorSetLayout for texture binding (binding 0: diffuse,
  binding 1: normal map combined image samplers)
- VkDescriptorPool sized for 256 descriptor sets
- Descriptor set cache keyed by (diffuseHandle, normalHandle)
- One-time command buffer helpers (beginOneTimeCommands/endOneTimeCommands)
- Full cleanup in cleanupTextureResources()

  - Plain-object guard error path

  - $dispose is idempotent (no throw on double-dispose)

  - Error propagation from synchronous and async actions via $onAction

* docs: phase 5 — documentation improvements from code review

- Document TimeTravelController API: entries, currentIndex, isReplaying,
  goTo() with a member-level table in both plugins.md and types.md

commit.message:
Migrate Kerncap to rocprof-sdk (#117)

* rocprofiler-sdk migration pass 1

Signed-off-by: coleramos425 <colramos@amd.com>

* fix(kerncap): strip @triton.autotune from standalone modules and synthesize missing config

The standalone extractor copied @triton.autotune decorators verbatim,
  but never extracted the module-level variables they reference (e.g.
  autotune_configs), causing NameError on import. Strip autotune
  decorators and keep only @triton.jit so the reproducer can call the
  bare JITFunction directly with pinned config kwargs.
  Also synthesize autotune_config from per-arg is_autotune_config flags
  when the top-level key is missing (warm autotuner cache), and pass
  autotune_stripped to the template so it skips the .fn unwrap on
  already-bare JITFunctions. Register a pyrepr Jinja2 filter (repr())
  so config values render as valid Python literals.

Signed-off-by: coleramos425 <colramos@amd.com>

* fix(kerncap): use pyrepr filter for autotune config kwargs in template

Replace tojson (warmup) and bare rendering (timed run) with pyrepr
  for autotune config kwargs. tojson rendered Python booleans as JSON
  false/true, crashing the reproducer with NameError. Bare rendering
  left strings unquoted. pyrepr (Python repr()) handles all types
  correctly. Also skip .fn unwrap when autotune was already stripped
  in the standalone extraction path.

Signed-off-by: coleramos425 <colramos@amd.com>

* test(kerncap): add unit tests for autotune stripping and config synthesis

Add fixture with multi-line @triton.autotune decorator and tests for:
  - autotune decorator stripping in standalone extraction
  - non-autotuned kernel passes through unchanged (regression guard)
  - autotune_config synthesized from per-arg is_autotune_config flags
  - non-standalone path still uses .fn bypass
  - explicit autotune_config in metadata takes precedence

fix: use ideal beat grid instead of aubio's noisy tracker (#22)

aubio's beat tracker produces irregular timestamps that drift up to
4.4s from the ideal grid by beat 29. Replace with a synthesized
evenly-spaced grid: beat[i] = offset + i * (60/BPM), using aubio's
first detected beat as the offset and its tempo detection for BPM.

Also allow gap=1 for medium/hard in level_designer since collision
timing now uses BeatInfo.arrival_time (BPM-independent).

Results for 2_drama (119.64 BPM):
- Easy: 108, Medium: 148, Hard: 160 obstacles
- Pro test player: 0 misses, 100% Perfect timing

commit.message:
fix: enable C++ exceptions for Windows ARM64 llama.cpp build

clang-cl targeting aarch64-pc-windows-msvc disables exceptions by
default, causing llama.cpp jinja code to fail with 'cannot use throw
with exceptions disabled'. The llama-cpp-sys-2 build.rs forwards
CMAKE_* env vars to CMake, so setting CMAKE_CXX_FLAGS=/EHsc fixes it.
